column2: Trigger (XYZ)
column3: Action (ABC)

column2: Maruti Suzuki launches e-VITARA; charging network maps released.
column3: Long MARUTI; pricing power in the service network is the "hidden" edge.

column2: Lithium prices drop 15% in Q1 due to oversupply from new African mines.
column3: Buy pure EV plays like Tesla (TSLA) or Tata Motors; margins will expand.

column2: Used car inventories hit a 2-year high; prices for 2022-24 models "crash."
column3: Short Carvana (CVNA) or used-car aggregators before Q1 earnings.

column2: Toyota (TM) leaks solid-state battery range of 1,000km for 2027 models.
column3: Buy TM on the rumor; this is a generational tech shift.

column2: Mahindra XUV 7XO (three-row) pre-bookings exceed 50k in 48 hours.
column3: Long M&M; it confirms the "mid-size SUV dominance" trend.

column2: Indian Govt increases FAME-III subsidies for 2-wheelers only.
column3: Rotate capital from car makers to TVS or Bajaj Auto.

column2: Nvidia (NVDA) signs "Automated Driving" chip deal with Hyundai.
column3: Long Hyundai (HYMTF); it’s becoming a "Software-Defined Vehicle" leader.

column2: Rubber prices spike due to floods in Southeast Asia.
column3: Short tire manufacturers like Apollo or MRF; input costs will eat profits.

column2: Ford announces exit from a high-loss European market.
column3: Buy the "Streamlining" news; stock usually rallies on cost-cutting.

column2: Rivian (RIVN) hits positive gross margin for the first time.
column3: Momentum Buy; the "death watch" for the startup is officially over.

column2: Ferrari (RACE) order book for the 2026 hybrid supercar is full through 2029.
column3: Hold for low-volatility growth; luxury is immune to inflation.

column2: Apple Pay integrates "Connected Car" micro-payments for tolls/gas.
column3: Buy stocks of dashboard OS providers like BlackBerry (BB/QNX).

column2: A major airbag recall is announced for 5 million Japanese vehicles.
column3: Short the specific OEM; look for a 3-5% "knee-jerk" drop.

column2: HDFC Bank cuts auto loan rates by 0.5%.
column3: Long mass-market OEMs like Maruti; lower EMI = higher volume.

column2: Lucid (LCID) receives an extra $2B from the Saudi PIF.
column3: Day Trade the news pop, but don't hold long; dilution is still a risk.

column2: Bosch announces a 30% reduction in EV powertrain weight.
column3: Buy Bosch (BOSCHLTD) as the "hidden winner" of the EV transition.

column2: BMW announces "Subscription-based" heated seats are being removed.
column3: Buy the sentiment shift; consumer-friendly moves improve brand loyalty.

column2: Oil falls below $65; consumer interest in "Hybrids" wanes.
column3: Short Toyota; Long pure-ICE (internal combustion) performance brands.

column2: Ola Electric reports 40% drop in service complaints.
column3: Long OLAELEC; the "quality" narrative is finally turning positive.

column2: BYD announces a $10k EV for the Latin American market.
column3: Short local budget competitors in those specific regions.
